assertion_results.text: Documentation includes all four HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE
assertion_results.evidence: All four HTTP methods documented with complete endpoint sections

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint has a human-readable description explaining what it does
assertion_results.evidence: Each endpoint has a clear, descriptive paragraph explaining purpose, behavior, and side effects

assertion_results.text: Path parameters (e.g., :id) are documented with their type and constraints
assertion_results.evidence: Path parameter 'id' documented as MongoId with format constraint and error message for invalid format

assertion_results.text: Query parameters for the list endpoint (page, limit) are documented with defaults
assertion_results.evidence: Complete parameter table: page (int, default 1, min 1), limit (int, default 25, 1-100), role (enum), search (string)

assertion_results.text: POST and PUT endpoints have request body schemas with field types and validation rules
assertion_results.evidence: Comprehensive body schemas with all Zod/validator constraints reflected

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint includes at least one response example with realistic JSON data
assertion_results.evidence: All endpoints have realistic response examples with proper data types and values

assertion_results.text: Error responses are documented with status codes (400, 404, 409, 422) and response bodies
assertion_results.evidence: Complete error tables with 404, 409, 422, 500 status codes and JSON response body examples

assertion_results.text: An OpenAPI 3.0 JSON spec is produced with valid paths and component schemas
assertion_results.evidence: Valid OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec with paths, component schemas, and proper $ref usage
